channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Syrian insurgents say they have entered Damascus as residents of capital report sounds of gunfire
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Syrian insurgents said early Sunday they had entered Damascus, capping a stunning advance across the country, as residents of the capital reported sounds of gunfire and explosions. There was no immediate official statement from the Syrian government.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and ZEINA KARAM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO assassination: Reports reveal contents of backpack linked to killer
item.description: Sources say that the gray backpack linked to the murder of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson contains a jacket, according to Bloomberg. NBC reported it also had Monopoly money.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Five California Lottery players become millionaires from Scratcher games
item.description: Five California Lottery players are the state’s newest millionaires after winning a combined total of $17 million from Scratcher games, lottery officials announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, ABC 4

item.title: Search for UnitedHealthcare CEO's killer yields evidence, but few answers
item.description: As the search for UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson’s killer stretches into a fourth day and beyond the New York City limits, investigators are reckoning with a tantalizing dichotomy.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: WATCH: Doorbell camera captures Illinois garbage truck explosion
item.description: Authorities described the explosion as "significant" and said truck parts were launched into the air, landing several blocks away.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says US should stay out of fighting in Syria as opposition forces gain ground
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump said Saturday that the U.S. military should stay out of the escalating conflict in Syria as a shock opposition offensive closes in on the capital, declaring in a social media post, "THIS IS NOT OUR FIGHT.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, WILL WEISSERT and TARA COPP, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 2 Pearl Harbor survivors, ages 104 and 102, return to Hawaii to honor those killed in 1941 attack
item.description: PEARL HARBOR, Hawaii (AP) — Ira “Ike” Schab, a 104-year-old Pearl Harbor attack survivor, was so determined to stand and salute during a remembrance ceremony honoring those killed in the Japanese bombing that thrust the U.S.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Willy Adames agrees to $182 million, 7-year deal with the Giants, AP source says
item.description: Willy Adames has agreed to a $182 million, seven-year contract with San Francisco, providing the Giants with a power-hitting shortstop in the prime of his career, according to a person familiar with the negotiations.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Executive of Tyler Perry Studios dies when plane he was piloting crashes in Florida
item.description: The president of Atlanta-based Tyler Perry Studios has died when the small plane he was piloting crashed on Florida’s Gulf Coast.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 5 recipes from the pros to give after-dinner cocktails some holiday flair
item.description: From a festive Old Fashioned to a boozy hot chocolate, these after-dinner drinks might inspire your own menu of holiday cocktails, to be sipped slowly by the fire or washed down with your Christmas pudding.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Miller High Life releases perfume that smells like a dive bar
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - If you've ever wondered how to describe the smell of your small local neighborhood bar, Miller High Life may have just provided that answer for you.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, KRON4

item.title: Florida prosecutor seeks to clear records of people charged with buying police-made crack in 1980s
item.description: A Florida prosecutor says he will seek to vacate as many as 2,600 convictions of people who bought crack cocaine manufactured by the Broward County Sheriff’s Office between 1988 and 1990.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Notre Dame reopens its doors to Macron and other world leaders in a rare symbol of unity
item.description: France's iconic Notre Dame Cathedral is formally reopening its doors on Saturday for the first time since a devastating fire nearly destroyed the 861-year-old landmark in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Adamson, KRON4

item.title: Austin Tice: Family of journalist kidnapped in Syria 12 years ago say he's still alive, citing ‘vetted' source
item.description: The family of Austin Tice, a former Marine turned journalist who was kidnapped by jihadist militants in 2012 while reporting in Syria, say he is still alive, citing a “significant source.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here are the billionaires stocking Trump’s next administration
item.description: President-elect Trump has named several billionaires to top roles in his next administration — a combination worth over $360 billion and that includes the world’s richest person.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Days after gunman killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO, police push to ID him and FBI offers reward
item.description: Nearly four days after the shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, police still do not know the gunman’s name or whereabouts or have a motive for the killing.
item.dc:creator: Michael Balsamo And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Recordings: Woman says slain Kentucky judge was involved in alleged sextortion scandal
item.description: In the recording, a woman claims to have seen video of Mullins engaging in sexual acts in his office.
item.dc:creator: Allison Hope Weiner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police find bag carried by gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO, say he likely fled NYC on bus
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The gunman who killed the CEO of the largest U.S. health insurer likely left New York City on a bus soon after the brazen ambush that has shaken corporate America, police officials said Friday. But he left something behind: a backpack that was discovered in Central Park.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: What to know about the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: The masked gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson remained elusive Friday, even as investigators learned more about his movements before and after the shocking crime. Investigators still don't know his name. They haven't found the gun.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man on trial in the killing of an Ole Miss student gave conflicting information, police say
item.description: The man on trial in the killing of University of Mississippi student Jimmie “Jay” Lee gave conflicting information to police about how well he and Lee knew each other.
item.dc:creator: Emily Wagster Pettus, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In California's 'earthquake country,' a 7.0 temblor prompts confusion and a tsunami warning
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Valerie Starkey was driving through Northern California to visit relatives when she suddenly felt shaking and feared her car had broken down, only to realize later that it was an earthquake so powerful that it triggered a tsunami warning for hundreds of miles of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge upholds use of race in Naval Academy admissions, saying a diverse military is stronger
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge on Friday ruled that the U.S. Naval Academy can continue considering race in its admissions process, finding that military cohesion and other national security factors mean the school should not be subjected to the same standards as civilian universities.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Crews recover the body of a woman from a Pennsylvania sinkhole after a 4-day search
item.description: The remains of a woman who fell into a sinkhole were recovered Friday, four days after she went missing while searching for her cat, a state police spokesperson said.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Indianapolis police officers are acquitted in the 2022 death of a man at his parents’ home
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Two Indianapolis police officers were acquitted of manslaughter and other charges Friday in the death of a man after officers shocked him with a Taser and restrained him face down while handcuffing him.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Body of missing grandmother who fell into sinkhole recovered after 4-day search
item.description: The search for 64-year-old Elizabeth Pollard, who fell into a sinkhole in Pittsburgh, has concluded after the coroner's office confirmed with news outlets that her body had been found.
item.dc:creator: Amber Baker, KRON4

item.title: USDA requires bulk testing of raw milk supply for bird flu virus
item.description: The nation’s milk supply must be tested for the bird flu virus H5N1 under a new order announced Friday by the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) – here's why.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Millions will lose health coverage if ACA subsidies expire: CBO
item.description: If Affordable Care Act subsidies are allowed to expire at the end of 2025 millions of people will lose their insurance and premiums will jump, according to a Congressional Budget Office report.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Teen killed, naked man arrested at California High School
item.description: A 24-year-old man, possibly naked and wielding an ax, was arrested in connection with the killing of a teenager in Newbury Park on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California's 'most popular' Christmas song, according to Google Trends data
item.description: Which Christmas songs do we just love to love, at least until we get sick of Christmas music and forget about it until late into the next calendar year? Google just might have the answer.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Judge urges NYC subway chokehold jury to keep deliberating after it struggles to reach verdict
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The judge in Daniel Penny’s subway chokehold manslaughter trial urged jurors on Friday to continue their deliberations after they told him they couldn't reach a unanimous vote on the top charge.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Walz says he was 'a little surprised' by election loss
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), Vice President Kamala Harris' running mate, reflected on the Democratic Party's loss in the presidential election, saying he was "a little surprised" by the outcome.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Massachusetts family receives holiday light tribute to late father
item.description: They're a tribute to cherished memories with a devoted husband and father who made the holiday season special.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Hegy, NewsNation
